wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the project overview section focused on the Ruicheng Educational Management System. Explain its purpose as a full-stack educational platform for question management, exam administration, and student learning assessment. Document the system architecture including FastAPI backend, React frontend, PostgreSQL database, and Docker deployment. Describe the multi-role user system (Student, Teacher, Question Admin, System Admin) and core functionality areas. Include both conceptual overviews for stakeholders and technical details for developers. Use terminology consistent with the educational management domain. Provide practical examples demonstrating the platform's capabilities in question management, exam creation, and learning analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the system architecture section focused on the Ruicheng Educational Management System's overall design. Explain the full-stack architecture including FastAPI backend with async/await patterns, React frontend with Ant Design components, PostgreSQL database with SQLAlchemy ORM, and Docker containerization. Document the layered architecture pattern with presentation, business logic, and data access layers. Describe the microservices approach, inter-component communication patterns, and data flow between frontend, backend, and database. Include system boundaries, external integrations (OCR, LLM services), and deployment topology. Provide architectural diagrams showing component relationships and data flow. Address scalability considerations, security architecture, and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive student user guide documentation for the Ruicheng Educational Management System. Document the complete student workflow including login procedures, dashboard navigation, question practice, exam taking processes, online answer submission, photo scanning for OCR, error book access and management, and typical question exploration. Include detailed step-by-step instructions with screenshots, explaining how students can browse available questions, take practice exams, submit answers either online or via photo upload, track their performance, access error books for weak areas, and utilize study recommendations. Address common student scenarios such as exam preparation, mistake analysis, progress tracking, and accessing study resources. Provide best practices for efficient studying, navigation tips, and troubleshooting guidance for typical student issues like login problems, submission errors, and performance tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Authentication API endpoints. Document JWT token-based authentication including login, logout, token refresh, and password reset functionality. Detail HTTP methods, URL patterns (/auth/login, /auth/logout, /auth/refresh), request/response schemas, and authentication headers. Include parameter specifications for username/password credentials, JWT token structure, and expiration handling. Document role-based access control with user permissions and token scopes. Provide practical examples with cURL commands showing successful authentication flows and error responses for invalid credentials. Address security considerations including HTTPS requirements, token storage, and session management. Include rate limiting policies and account lockout mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationship Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity relationship documentation for the Ruicheng Educational Management System database schema. Detail all 20+ database tables including their primary keys, foreign key relationships, and cardinality constraints. Document the complete ER diagram showing relationships between users (students, admins, sys_admins), educational content (questions, syllabi, knowledge nodes), assessment components (exam papers, answer submissions), and system components (notifications, ML models). Explain the hierarchical relationships in knowledge trees, many-to-many associations in class enrollment and question-knowledge mapping, and audit trail relationships. Include table dependency order for proper database initialization, referential integrity rules, and cascading delete/update behaviors. Document the UUID-based primary key strategy and its benefits for distributed systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed component architecture documentation for the React 19 application. Document the component hierarchy starting from AppLayout as the main layout wrapper, explaining how pages are structured and composed. Detail the component composition patterns, prop passing strategies, and component reusability principles. Explain the page-based organization where each major feature has dedicated page components. Document the layout system, responsive design patterns, and component styling approaches. Include guidelines for creating new components, component naming conventions, and folder structure organization. Address component lifecycle management, performance optimization through component splitting, and accessibility considerations in component design.

wiki_catalogs.name: Question CRUD Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for question CRUD operations covering create, read, update, and delete functionality. Document the QuestionCreate and QuestionUpdate schemas, validation rules, and field requirements. Explain the permission system for different user roles (TEACHER, QUESTION_ADMIN, SYS_ADMIN) and their access levels. Cover the question lifecycle from creation through deletion, including ownership verification and administrative overrides. Detail the frontend interfaces for question listing, editing, and form validation. Include examples of successful operations, error handling scenarios, and role-based access restrictions. Address the relationship between questions and their creators, update tracking, and soft deletion patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Exam Paper Creation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for exam paper creation functionality. Document the complete workflow for creating new exam papers including the ExamPaperCreate schema, validation rules, and automatic question import capabilities. Explain the paper metadata structure (title, subtitle, subject, grade level, duration, total score), instruction management, and status initialization. Cover the frontend modal interface for paper creation, form validation, and real-time preview functionality. Include examples of paper creation workflows, bulk question import scenarios, and best practices for paper organization. Address paper duplication prevention, creator permissions, and the relationship between papers and their associated questions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Online Answer Submission
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the online answer submission system. Document the complete workflow from exam selection to answer submission, including the AnswerSubmissionCreate schema, AnswerSubmissionResponse model, and AnswerDetail handling. Explain the submission validation process, question existence checking, and automatic grading integration. Cover the frontend OnlineAnswerTab interface for real-time answer taking, form validation, and submission handling. Detail the database transactions for atomic submission creation, answer detail insertion, and auto-grading execution. Include examples of submission requests, response formats, and error handling scenarios. Address the integration with the grading engine and mistake book generation triggers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FastAPI application structure. Explain the main application entry point, router organization, and modular API endpoint grouping. Document the project configuration system, environment variable management, and settings hierarchy. Detail the application initialization process, startup events, and health check mechanisms. Include the routing strategy, URL pattern organization, and namespace separation. Address the modular architecture approach, dependency injection patterns, and application lifecycle management. Provide guidelines for adding new API modules, organizing endpoint handlers, and maintaining clean code structure.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Detection Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the error detection algorithms section. Document the automatic error detection mechanisms that analyze student answer submissions to identify mistakes. Explain the pattern matching algorithms used to compare student responses with correct answers, including support for different question types (single choice, multiple choice, fill blank, subjective). Detail the scoring thresholds, tolerance levels for subjective answers, and the integration with OCR processing for scanned answers. Include the frequency analysis algorithms that identify recurring error patterns and the machine learning components for intelligent error classification. Provide examples of detection logic, threshold configurations, and integration with the assessment workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Class Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the class management system covering teacher class administration, student enrollment, and course organization. Document the class creation workflow, student roster management, and class assignment processes. Explain the class hierarchy structure, grade level organization, and subject allocation features. Include class settings configuration, academic year management, and class status controls. Address student registration procedures, class transfer processes, and enrollment validation. Document the frontend class management interface, bulk operations, and administrative controls. Provide examples of class setup workflows, student onboarding processes, and class maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system configuration documentation covering LLM configuration management, system settings, and AI service integration. Document the LLM configuration interface, model parameter management, and AI service endpoints. Explain the configuration validation, deployment settings, and runtime configuration updates. Include the system-wide settings management, environment variable configuration, and service discovery mechanisms. Address configuration backup, restore procedures, and version management. Provide examples of LLM model setup, configuration workflows, and system tuning procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: OCR Engine Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive OCR engine integration documentation covering the Tesseract OCR implementation for answer sheet processing. Document the OCR service architecture, image preprocessing pipeline, and text recognition workflow. Explain the question extraction algorithm, confidence scoring mechanism, and heuristic parsing for different question types. Include configuration options for Tesseract languages, image processing parameters, and threshold settings. Provide examples of OCR processing workflows, error handling strategies, and integration with the grading system. Document the structured output format, confidence estimation methods, and manual review processes for low-confidence results.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Dashboard API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Student Dashboard API endpoints. Document the /student/stats endpoint that provides comprehensive student analytics and progress tracking. Detail the HTTP GET method, URL pattern /student/stats, and authentication requirements. Include parameter specifications for current user context and database session dependencies. Document the response schema covering completed_papers count, accuracy_rate percentage, error_count from error notebooks, highest_score achieved, recent_papers array with exam details, and subject_distribution statistics. Provide practical examples with JSON responses showing student performance metrics. Address database query optimizations, filtering by submission status, and data aggregation techniques used for dashboard calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Curriculum Mapping
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive curriculum mapping documentation covering the syllabus management system and knowledge tree structure. Document the syllabus creation workflow, version control mechanisms, and curriculum standardization processes. Explain the hierarchical knowledge tree organization, node types (CHAPTER, SECTION, POINT), and relationship management. Include the syllabus versioning system with rollback capabilities, content inheritance, and modification tracking. Address the integration with subject-specific curricula, grade-level alignment, and geographic province variations. Document the curriculum design principles, content validation rules, and standardization workflows. Provide examples of syllabus creation, version management, and curriculum mapping best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Docker Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Docker configuration documentation covering container setup, image building, and orchestration. Document the Docker Compose configuration including service definitions, network setup, volume mounts, and environment variable management. Explain the backend and frontend Dockerfile configurations, build contexts, and multi-stage builds. Detail the container networking, port mappings, and inter-service communication. Include examples of customizing Docker configurations for different environments, security considerations for container isolation, and best practices for image optimization. Address container health checks, resource limits, and restart policies. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common Docker deployment issues and performance tuning recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Ruicheng Educational Management System. Include prerequisites (Python 3.9+, Node.js, Docker, PostgreSQL), development environment setup, database configuration, and initial project launch procedures. Document the complete installation process from repository cloning through to first successful run. Provide step-by-step instructions for both development and production environments. Include environment variable configuration, database seeding, and initial user account setup. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Make content accessible to developers with varying experience levels while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the technology stack section focused on the Ruicheng Educational Management System's technical foundation. Document the backend stack including FastAPI 0.104.1, SQLAlchemy 2.0 (async), Alembic migrations, Pydantic validation, and PostgreSQL 16. Detail the frontend stack with React 19.2, Ant Design 6.4, TypeScript 6.0, and Zustand state management. Explain infrastructure components including Docker, Docker Compose, and container orchestration. Document key libraries and frameworks such as PaddleOCR for OCR processing, Ollama/vLLM for LLM integration, and MinIO for file storage. Include version compatibility matrices, dependency relationships, and upgrade considerations. Provide rationale for technology choices and alternatives considered.

wiki_catalogs.name: Teacher Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive teacher user guide documentation for the Ruicheng Educational Management System. Document the complete teacher workflow including login procedures, class management, exam paper creation and administration, student performance analytics, question statistics analysis, and class progress monitoring. Include detailed step-by-step instructions with screenshots, explaining how teachers can manage their classes, create and customize exam papers, assign questions, monitor student performance in real-time, analyze question difficulty and student understanding, export performance reports, and track individual student progress. Address common teacher scenarios such as preparing exams, analyzing class performance, identifying knowledge gaps, creating personalized learning paths, and generating comprehensive analytics reports. Provide best practices for effective classroom management, exam administration, performance analysis, and student engagement strategies. Include troubleshooting guidance for typical teacher issues like paper creation errors, analytics display problems, and class management challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for User Management endpoints covering student, teacher, and administrator user operations. Document CRUD operations for user profiles, enrollment management, and role assignments. Detail HTTP methods, URL patterns (/student/, /teacher/, /admin/), request/response schemas for user registration, profile updates, and status management. Include parameter specifications for user data validation, role-based permissions, and access controls. Document student-specific features like class enrollment, progress tracking, and exam scheduling. Cover teacher functionality including class management, student performance monitoring, and assignment creation. Address administrator operations for user administration, system configuration, and audit trails. Provide practical examples with JSON payloads and error handling scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and Schemas
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed data model documentation for all database entities in the Ruicheng Educational Management System. Document each SQLAlchemy model class including field definitions, data types, constraints, and validation rules. Explain the JSONB field usage for flexible metadata storage in questions, syllabi, and knowledge nodes. Detail the polymorphic inheritance patterns used in user models (Student, Admin, SysAdmin). Document the composite unique constraints, check constraints, and business rule validations. Include field-level documentation for audit trails (created_at, updated_at), status tracking fields, and relationship indicators. Explain the model-to-database mapping, column-level comments, and index recommendations. Provide examples of model instantiation, relationship access patterns, and query optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed state management documentation for the Zustand-based state solution. Document the authentication store implementation including user session management, JWT token handling, and role-based access control state. Explain the store structure, actions, and selectors used throughout the application. Detail the integration with the API client for automatic token refresh and authentication state synchronization. Document state persistence strategies, state hydration from server responses, and error state management. Include best practices for store composition, avoiding unnecessary re-renders, and debugging state changes. Address the useReferenceValues hook pattern and how it integrates with global state management for reference data caching.

wiki_catalogs.name: Question Types and Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for question types and validation systems. Detail the supported question types (SINGLE_CHOICE, MULTIPLE_CHOICE, FILL_BLANK, SUBJECTIVE) with their specific field requirements and validation rules. Explain the correct_answer field structure for different question types, including multiple correct answers for multiple choice questions. Document the difficulty scoring system, subject categorization, and grade level specifications. Cover the meta_data field for knowledge point mapping and custom attributes. Include validation examples for each question type, field constraints, and data integrity checks. Address the relationship between question type and answer validation, scoring algorithms, and display formatting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Question Assignment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for question assignment within exam papers. Detail the question addition workflow, position management, and scoring assignment per question. Explain the exam_paper_questions association table structure, question ordering algorithms, and position-based sorting mechanisms. Document the question filtering and search capabilities, knowledge point mapping, and difficulty distribution management. Cover the frontend drag-and-drop interface for question arrangement, real-time position updates, and bulk question operations. Include examples of question assignment strategies, randomization techniques, and best practices for creating balanced exam papers. Address question removal, reordering, and the impact on paper statistics.

wiki_catalogs.name: OCR Processing Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the OCR processing system integrated with answer submission. Document the OCR upload workflow, image preprocessing pipeline, and text extraction algorithms using PaddleOCR. Explain the OCRUpload model, file validation, and storage integration with MinIO object storage. Detail the frontend PhotoScanTab interface for image capture/upload, preview functionality, and processing status tracking. Cover the OCR service architecture, batch processing capabilities, and error handling for poor image quality. Include examples of supported image formats, resolution requirements, and processing timeouts. Address the integration between OCR results and the grading system, including confidence scoring and manual override capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the service layer architecture and business logic implementation. Explain the service pattern implementation, dependency injection, and service composition. Document the OCR service integration, LLM service orchestration, and grading engine functionality. Detail the mistake detection service, captcha generation service, and external API integrations. Include service lifecycle management, error handling strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Address service testing approaches, mocking strategies, and service boundary definitions. Provide guidelines for implementing new services, maintaining service isolation, and ensuring proper error propagation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Manual Entry Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the manual error entry process. Detail the student-driven manual entry workflow through the web interface, including the form-based input system for capturing question details, student answers, and error classifications. Document the frontend components that enable manual mistake logging, the validation rules applied during entry, and the integration with the error notebook generation process. Explain the teacher and administrator manual correction workflows, including bulk editing capabilities and approval processes. Include step-by-step user guides, form field descriptions, error handling during manual entry, and the relationship between manual entries and automated error detection results.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the performance analytics system covering student assessment analysis, statistical reporting, and comparative evaluation tools. Document the paper statistics dashboard, exam performance metrics, and aggregate reporting features. Explain the question statistics system, item analysis, and difficulty distribution reporting. Include grade distribution analysis, cohort comparison tools, and trend visualization capabilities. Address the statistical calculation algorithms, data aggregation methods, and reporting filters. Document the analytics interface, interactive charts, and export functionality. Provide examples of performance interpretation, instructional decision-making, and student intervention strategies based on analytics insights.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed user management documentation covering administrative user accounts, role assignment, and permission management. Document the admin user creation workflow, role-based access control, and user lifecycle management. Explain the teacher and question administrator account management, student enrollment processes, and class assignment workflows. Include the user profile management, authentication configuration, and account status controls. Address bulk user operations, user import/export functionality, and audit trail management. Provide examples of user administration procedures, role assignment workflows, and account maintenance tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Service Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed LLM service integration documentation covering the intelligent grading and content analysis capabilities. Document the LLM configuration management, model selection, and prompt engineering strategies. Explain the automated grading workflows, content analysis pipelines, and intelligent tutoring system features. Include configuration examples for different LLM providers, API integration patterns, and cost optimization strategies. Provide guidelines for custom prompt development, evaluation metrics, and quality assurance processes. Document the relationship between LLM services and the grading engine, including fallback mechanisms and human review workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Knowledge Node Trees
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed knowledge node tree documentation covering the hierarchical organization of educational content. Document the KnowledgeNode model structure, node relationships, and tree traversal algorithms. Explain the node type system (CHAPTER, SECTION, POINT), parent-child relationships, and tree manipulation operations. Include the node activation/deactivation system, modification tracking, and invalidation mechanisms. Address the tree serialization format, nested structure representation, and recursive operations. Document the node sorting system, order management, and tree rebuilding processes. Provide examples of node creation, tree navigation, bulk operations, and tree maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed production deployment documentation covering the complete deployment pipeline and operational procedures. Document the deployment script execution, environment preparation, and step-by-step deployment workflow. Explain the database migration process using Alembic, including migration execution, rollback procedures, and version management. Detail the production environment configuration, secrets management, and security hardening. Include CI/CD pipeline integration, automated testing during deployment, and rollback strategies. Address load balancing, SSL/TLS configuration, and reverse proxy setup. Document the deployment checklist, pre-deployment validation, and post-deployment verification procedures. Provide examples of deployment commands, configuration templates, and operational runbooks for system administrators.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive user guide documentation covering all user roles and their workflows in the Ruicheng Educational Management System. Document the complete user journey for Students (practice questions, exam taking, error book access), Teachers (class management, exam creation, analytics), Question Administrators (content review and approval), and System Administrators (system configuration and user management). Include detailed screenshots, step-by-step instructions, and best practices for each role. Address common user scenarios, navigation patterns, and feature discovery. Provide troubleshooting guidance for typical user issues and explain the rationale behind key interface decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the core features overview section focused on the Ruicheng Educational Management System's primary functionality domains. Explain the four main feature areas: Question Management (CRUD operations, batch import/export, review workflow), Exam Administration (paper creation, question assignment, status management), Student Assessment (online submission, OCR processing, automated grading), and Error Book System (automatic detection, manual entry, classification). Document the multi-role user system enabling different workflows for Students, Teachers, Question Administrators, and System Administrators. Describe the knowledge management system including curriculum mapping and syllabus management. Include feature interdependencies, data relationships, and user workflow patterns. Provide practical examples of typical use cases and system capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Question Administrator Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive question administrator user guide documentation for the Ruicheng Educational Management System. Document the complete question administrator workflow including login procedures, question management, batch import/export operations, content review and approval processes, knowledge tree maintenance, and quality assurance procedures. Include detailed step-by-step instructions with screenshots, explaining how question administrators can create and edit questions, perform bulk question imports using CSV templates, review and approve submitted content, manage question categories and difficulty levels, map questions to knowledge points, maintain curriculum alignment, and ensure content quality standards. Address common administrator scenarios such as bulk content processing, content verification workflows, knowledge structure updates, question categorization, and maintaining educational standards. Provide best practices for content management, quality control procedures, knowledge mapping strategies, and administrative efficiency. Include troubleshooting guidance for typical administrator issues like import errors, approval workflows, content validation problems, and knowledge tree maintenance challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Question Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Question Management endpoints covering question CRUD operations, batch import/export, review workflows, and knowledge point mapping. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/questions/, /question-admin/), request/response schemas for question data operations. Detail parameter specifications for question types (single choice, multiple choice, fill blank, subjective), difficulty levels, subject associations, and knowledge node relationships. Include batch operations for question import/export with CSV/JSON formats. Document review and approval workflows, status management, and quality control processes. Provide practical examples with JSON payloads for question creation, bulk operations, and administrative review processes. Address question validation rules, duplicate detection, and content moderation requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive migration management documentation for the Alembic-based database versioning system. Detail the migration workflow from initial schema creation through incremental updates. Document each migration file including the upgrade/downgrade functions, table creation sequences, and data transformation logic. Explain the semantic versioning approach with revision IDs (001_v22_initial, 002_add_provinces_table, etc.). Detail the migration execution commands, environment configuration, and rollback procedures. Document the relationship between migration files and database state evolution. Include best practices for writing safe migrations, handling data preservation during schema changes, and managing production deployments. Explain the Alembic configuration, target metadata, and environment-specific settings. Address common migration issues, conflict resolution, and testing strategies for database schema changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive routing and navigation documentation for the React Router configuration. Document the route structure including public routes (login), protected routes with role-based access, and nested route organization. Explain the navigation patterns, breadcrumb implementation, and active link highlighting. Detail the route guards for authentication and authorization, redirect logic for different user roles, and dynamic route generation. Document the navigation menu structure, sidebar organization, and mobile-responsive navigation patterns. Include guidelines for adding new routes, route parameter handling, query string management, and programmatic navigation. Address navigation performance optimization, lazy loading strategies, and SEO considerations for single-page application routing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Batch Import and Export
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for batch import and export functionality. Document the batch_import_questions endpoint with JSON array processing, field mapping, and validation rules. Explain the export_selected and export_questions endpoints with filtering capabilities and export limits. Cover the maximum import/export limits (200 items), data transformation processes, and error handling for batch operations. Detail the frontend batch import modal interface, CSV/JSON format requirements, and validation feedback. Include examples of import templates, export filters, and troubleshooting common import/export issues. Address performance considerations, batch size limits, and data consistency during bulk operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Exam Publishing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the exam publishing workflow and status management. Document the transition from draft to published state, including validation requirements, prerequisite checks, and publishing restrictions. Explain the paper preview functionality, export capabilities (Word, PDF), and print preparation features. Cover the frontend publishing interface, status indicators, and administrative controls. Include examples of publishing workflows, quality assurance checks, and rollback procedures. Address the relationship between paper status and student access, timing controls, and exam scheduling integration. Document the export formats, styling options, and print optimization features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Automated Grading Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the automated grading engine system. Document the grade_answer function implementation, question type handling for multiple choice, fill-in-the-blank, and subjective questions. Explain the scoring algorithm, partial credit calculation, and feedback generation for different question types. Detail the GradingRecord model for audit trails, including model version tracking, processing timestamps, and detailed score breakdowns. Cover the integration with the judge engine service, score mapping from exam paper configurations, and grade calculation methodology. Include examples of grading workflows, score calculation formulas, and feedback templates. Address the grading audit system, performance monitoring, and fallback mechanisms for edge cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Middleware and Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for middleware configuration and security implementation. Explain the JWT authentication system, token validation, and refresh mechanisms. Document the role-based access control (RBAC) implementation, permission checking, and authorization decorators. Detail the CORS middleware configuration, response formatting middleware, and request preprocessing. Include security best practices, input validation, and protection against common vulnerabilities. Address middleware ordering, custom middleware development, and security audit considerations. Provide guidelines for implementing new security measures, extending authentication methods, and maintaining security compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Classification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the error classification system. Document the taxonomy of error types including concept errors, calculation mistakes, reading comprehension issues, and knowledge gaps. Explain the classification algorithms that automatically categorize detected errors, the manual classification options available to teachers and administrators, and the hierarchical structure of error categories. Detail the frequency-based classification that identifies patterns in student performance, the integration with knowledge node mapping for curriculum alignment, and the statistical analysis used to group similar errors. Include classification criteria, category definitions, automated assignment rules, and manual override capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Progress Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the student progress tracking system covering individual learning assessment, mastery monitoring, and academic development analysis. Document the student performance monitoring interface, progress visualization tools, and learning trajectory tracking. Explain the assessment history system, improvement indicators, and milestone achievement tracking. Include the error book integration for learning gap identification, skill mastery indicators, and personalized learning recommendations. Address the progress reporting mechanisms, individual student profiles, and comparative analysis tools. Document the tracking dashboard, filtering options, and intervention support features. Provide examples of progress interpretation, targeted instruction strategies, and student support planning based on tracking data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Knowledge Tree Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive knowledge tree management documentation covering curriculum mapping, knowledge node organization, and syllabus administration. Document the knowledge tree structure, node hierarchy management, and point-to-node relationships. Explain the syllabus creation workflow, curriculum alignment, and content organization procedures. Include the knowledge point classification, prerequisite relationships, and learning pathway design. Address bulk content import, tree visualization tools, and curriculum versioning. Provide examples of knowledge tree setup, syllabus configuration, and content mapping workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Storage Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive file storage integration documentation covering media management, file upload/download workflows, and cloud storage configurations. Document the storage service architecture, file handling mechanisms, and content delivery optimization. Explain supported storage backends, file format handling, and security considerations for file access. Include configuration examples for local storage, cloud storage providers, and CDN integration. Provide guidelines for file organization, metadata management, and backup strategies. Document the integration with OCR and grading workflows, including temporary file management and cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Syllabus Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed syllabus management documentation covering the complete syllabus lifecycle and administration system. Document the Syllabus model with grade levels, provinces, subjects, and content structures. Explain the syllabus versioning system, parent-child relationships for version chains, and rollback mechanisms. Include the syllabus creation workflow, editing processes, and approval workflows. Address the integration with knowledge trees, content synchronization, and version propagation. Document the syllabus search and filtering capabilities, status management, and administrative controls. Provide examples of syllabus creation, version management, content updates, and administrative operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring & Logging
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive monitoring and logging documentation covering system observability, metrics collection, and log management. Document the integration with monitoring tools like Prometheus, Grafana, and ELK stack. Provide troubleshooting workflows using logs and metrics, performance bottleneck identification, and capacity planning based on monitoring data.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Ruicheng Educational Management System RESTful API. Document all HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint group. Include detailed parameter specifications, return value formats, and error codes. Cover authentication endpoints (JWT token management), user management APIs, question management operations, exam administration functions, student assessment workflows, and administrative tools. Provide practical examples with cURL commands and JSON payloads. Document rate limiting, pagination, filtering, and sorting capabilities. Include security considerations, CORS configuration, and API versioning strategy. Address common integration patterns and troubleshooting approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Roles & Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the user roles and permissions section focused on the Ruicheng Educational Management System's multi-role authentication and authorization model. Document the four distinct user roles: Student (access to question lists, exam taking, error book access), Teacher (class management, exam creation, performance analytics), Question Administrator (question review and approval workflow), and System Administrator (system configuration and user management). Explain the JWT-based authentication system, role-based access control implementation, and permission inheritance patterns. Detail the user registration process, password security measures, and session management. Include role transition scenarios, delegation capabilities, and administrative oversight features. Document security considerations, audit logging, and compliance requirements for each role type.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Administrator Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system administrator user guide documentation for the Ruicheng Educational Management System. Document the complete system administrator workflow including secure login procedures, user management, system configuration, basic settings management, syllabus administration, and system monitoring capabilities. Include detailed step-by-step instructions with screenshots, explaining how system administrators can manage user accounts and permissions, configure system-wide settings, maintain syllabus structures, oversee system performance, manage administrative users, configure system parameters, and monitor system health. Address common administrator scenarios such as user provisioning and deprovisioning, system configuration changes, permission management, backup and recovery procedures, system updates, and troubleshooting system-wide issues. Provide best practices for system security, user management, configuration management, performance optimization, and disaster recovery planning. Include troubleshooting guidance for typical system administrator issues like user access problems, configuration errors, system performance issues, and administrative account management challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Exam Administration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for Exam Administration endpoints covering exam paper creation, question assignment, exam scheduling, and status management. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/exam-papers/), request/response schemas for exam paper operations. Detail parameter specifications for exam configurations, question selection algorithms, timing constraints, and publishing workflows. Include exam paper templates, question randomization, and exam duration settings. Document exam status transitions (draft, published, active, completed), student enrollment, and exam monitoring. Provide practical examples with JSON payloads for exam creation, question assignment, and exam administration. Address exam security measures, proctoring integrations, and result processing workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Performance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive database performance documentation for the Ruicheng Educational Management System. Detail the PostgreSQL optimization strategies including index creation patterns, query optimization techniques, and connection pooling configuration. Document the UUID-based primary key strategy advantages for distributed environments and its impact on performance. Explain the JSONB field indexing strategies for flexible metadata queries and search operations. Detail the async database session management, connection pool sizing, and transaction isolation levels. Document performance monitoring approaches, query profiling techniques, and bottleneck identification methods. Include recommendations for read replica setup, partitioning strategies for large datasets, and caching integration patterns. Address database maintenance tasks, vacuum/analyze procedures, and backup optimization. Explain the performance implications of the knowledge tree structure, many-to-many relationships, and concurrent user access patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed build configuration documentation for the Vite-based React application. Document the Vite configuration including plugins, build optimization settings, and development server configuration. Explain the TypeScript configuration, compiler options, and type checking setup. Detail the ESLint configuration for code quality enforcement, import sorting, and React-specific linting rules. Document the package.json scripts for development, build, and deployment workflows. Include build optimization strategies such as code splitting, tree shaking, and asset optimization. Address environment variable handling, proxy configuration for API requests, and hot module replacement setup. Document the development workflow, debugging configuration, and production build optimization including bundle analysis and performance monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Review and Approval Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the question review and approval workflow system. Detail the review_status field values (APPROVED, PENDING, REJECTED) and their implications. Explain the role-based approval process involving TEACHER, QUESTION_ADMIN, and SYS_ADMIN permissions. Document the typical question marking system with is_typical flag and its impact on question visibility. Cover the content review process, quality assurance checks, and administrative oversight mechanisms. Include examples of approval workflows, rejection reasons, and re-submission processes. Address the relationship between review status and question availability in exams, the typical question system for teacher recommendations, and audit trails for review actions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Exam Status Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for exam status tracking and management throughout the exam lifecycle. Document the status enumeration (Draft, Published, Completed) and state transition rules. Explain the submission status management for individual students, including GENERATED, RE_GRADED, and completion tracking. Cover the relationship between paper status and submission status, audit trails, and historical tracking. Detail the frontend status indicators, progress monitoring, and administrative oversight features. Include examples of status workflows, bulk status updates, and reporting capabilities. Address the integration with answer submission system, grading workflows, and completion analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Manual Review Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the manual review and correction process. Document the teacher interface for reviewing student submissions, correcting scores, and updating answer details. Explain the manual grading workflow, including the ability to override automated scores, add custom feedback, and adjust point allocations. Detail the AnswerDetail model modifications for manual corrections, including is_correct flags, score adjustments, and feedback fields. Cover the teacher dashboard integration for bulk review operations, selective correction workflows, and grade reconciliation processes. Include examples of manual review scenarios, correction procedures, and quality assurance checks. Address the audit trail preservation during manual reviews and the impact on overall exam statistics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for database session management and ORM configuration. Explain the SQLAlchemy async session configuration, connection pooling, and transaction management. Document the declarative base class, model inheritance patterns, and relationship definitions. Detail the database initialization process, session lifecycle, and proper resource cleanup. Include error handling strategies for database operations, connection recovery, and performance optimization. Address database migration management, Alembic integration, and schema evolution. Provide guidelines for implementing new models, optimizing queries, and maintaining database consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Study Recommendation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the study recommendation engine. Detail the AI-powered recommendation algorithms that suggest targeted practice questions based on error patterns and learning objectives. Explain the LLM integration for generating personalized practice questions, the knowledge node mapping that aligns recommendations with curriculum standards, and the adaptive learning path generation. Document the recommendation scoring system that prioritizes questions based on error frequency, difficulty level, and learning gap analysis. Include the practice question generation workflow, quality assurance processes for recommended questions, and integration with the self-study scheduling system. Address recommendation personalization, algorithm transparency, and performance optimization for large-scale recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed system monitoring documentation covering system health tracking, performance metrics, and maintenance operations. Document the database monitoring tools, storage management, and system resource tracking. Explain the performance analytics, error tracking, and system alert mechanisms. Include the maintenance workflows, backup procedures, and system diagnostics. Address the monitoring dashboard, log analysis tools, and troubleshooting procedures. Provide examples of system health checks, performance optimization workflows, and maintenance scheduling procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed security services documentation covering CAPTCHA integration, authentication mechanisms, and protection against spam and abuse. Document the CAPTCHA service implementation, verification workflows, and integration with user registration/login processes. Explain the security middleware, rate limiting strategies, and anti-abuse measures. Include configuration examples for CAPTCHA providers, security headers, and access control policies. Provide guidelines for implementing custom security measures, monitoring suspicious activities, and maintaining system integrity. Document the relationship between security services and user authentication, including session management and token validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Organization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content organization documentation covering the mapping between knowledge nodes and learning objectives. Document the KnowledgePoint system, competency frameworks, and learning outcome alignment. Explain the knowledge point creation, categorization, and relationship mapping processes. Include the integration with question bank organization, exam preparation workflows, and learning path generation. Address the content standardization processes, taxonomy systems, and cross-referencing mechanisms. Document the knowledge point validation, quality assurance, and content review workflows. Provide examples of knowledge point creation, content mapping, competency assessment, and learning objective alignment.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed system maintenance documentation covering database migrations, updates, backups, and routine maintenance procedures. Document the database migration strategy using Alembic, including creating new migrations, applying changes, and maintaining migration history. Explain the backup and recovery procedures for databases, file storage, and configuration data. Detail the update procedures for both backend and frontend components, including zero-downtime deployments and rollback processes. Include routine maintenance tasks such as log cleanup, database optimization, and performance tuning. Address security patches, dependency updates, and vulnerability management. Document the maintenance schedule, change management procedures, and incident response protocols. Provide examples of maintenance scripts, backup verification procedures, and disaster recovery testing scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database design documentation for the Ruicheng Educational Management System. Detail the complete entity-relationship model including all tables, relationships, foreign keys, and constraints. Document the PostgreSQL schema with JSONB fields for flexible metadata storage. Explain the migration strategy using Alembic, version control approach, and data seeding processes. Include database indexing strategy, performance considerations, and query optimization patterns. Document the reference data system and master data management. Address data integrity rules, cascading operations, and audit trail mechanisms. Provide ER diagrams, table schemas, and sample data examples. Include backup strategies, data lifecycle management, and compliance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Assessment API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Student Assessment endpoints covering online answer submission, OCR processing, automated grading, and score calculation. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/answers/, /ocr/, /grading/), request/response schemas for assessment operations. Detail parameter specifications for answer submission formats, OCR image processing, LLM-based scoring, and grade calculation algorithms. Include answer validation, plagiarism detection, and score verification processes. Document OCR integration with PaddleOCR, image upload handling, and processing workflows. Provide practical examples with JSON payloads for answer submission, OCR processing, and grading results. Address assessment security, anti-cheating measures, and result auditing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Plugin & Extension Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive plugin and extension architecture documentation covering the extensibility mechanisms, service registration patterns, and custom extension development. Document the plugin framework, service discovery mechanisms, and dependency injection patterns. Explain how to develop custom services, integrate third-party APIs, and extend existing functionality. Include examples of plugin development, service registration, and configuration management. Provide guidelines for creating custom deduplication services, scraping integrations, and grading engines. Document the service lifecycle management, error handling strategies, and performance optimization techniques for extensible systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for the React 19 TypeScript application. Describe the component hierarchy, routing configuration with React Router, and state management using Zustand. Document the Ant Design integration, styling strategies, and responsive design patterns. Explain the API client architecture, authentication flow, and component composition patterns. Include build configuration with Vite, TypeScript configuration, and development workflow. Address component reusability, prop drilling solutions, and performance optimization techniques. Document the layout system, theme management, and internationalization considerations. Provide guidelines for component development, testing strategies, and deployment pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Book API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for Error Book endpoints covering automatic error detection, manual error entry, error classification, and study recommendations. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/error-notebooks/), request/response schemas for error book operations. Detail parameter specifications for error categorization, difficulty analysis, knowledge gap identification, and personalized study plans. Include error book generation algorithms, manual correction workflows, and export functionality. Document error statistics, trend analysis, and progress tracking features. Provide practical examples with JSON payloads for error book creation, manual corrections, and report generation. Address error book privacy, data retention, and learning analytics integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend architecture documentation for the FastAPI application. Describe the layered architecture pattern, dependency injection, and middleware configuration. Document the service layer design, repository pattern implementation, and database session management. Explain the authentication and authorization system, JWT token handling, and role-based access control. Include error handling strategies, response formatting, and CORS configuration. Address middleware chain, request/response processing, and exception handling. Document the service orchestration, external API integrations, and background task processing. Provide guidelines for adding new endpoints, implementing business logic, and maintaining code quality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Knowledge Tree API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Knowledge Tree endpoints covering curriculum mapping, knowledge node management, syllabus organization, and content hierarchy. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/knowledge-tree/), request/response schemas for knowledge tree operations. Detail parameter specifications for knowledge node relationships, prerequisite mappings, skill hierarchies, and curriculum alignment. Include knowledge tree visualization, node editing, and content organization workflows. Document syllabus management, course planning, and learning pathway creation. Provide practical examples with JSON payloads for knowledge tree construction, node relationships, and curriculum mapping. Address knowledge tree versioning, content validation, and educational standards compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation for the multi-role educational management system. Detail the JWT-based authentication flow, token generation and validation, and refresh token mechanisms. Document the role-based access control system with four distinct user roles (Student, Teacher, Question Admin, System Admin) and their permission hierarchies. Explain the password hashing, session management, and security middleware implementation. Include login/logout flows, password reset procedures, and account management features. Address security best practices, CSRF protection, and secure cookie handling. Provide implementation examples for protected routes, role checking utilities, and permission validation. Document integration with frontend authentication state management and automatic token refresh mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Configuration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for System Configuration endpoints covering LLM configuration, database management, reference data, and self-study scheduling. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/admin/llm/, /database/, /reference/, /self-study/), request/response schemas for system configuration operations. Detail parameter specifications for LLM model settings, database connection management, reference data maintenance, and self-study algorithm configuration. Include system health monitoring, backup operations, and maintenance workflows. Document reference data management for subjects, knowledge nodes, and system constants. Provide practical examples with JSON payloads for configuration updates, system diagnostics, and maintenance operations. Address system security, backup procedures, and operational monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Question Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive question management system documentation covering the complete lifecycle of educational questions. Document CRUD operations for all question types (Single Choice, Multiple Choice, Fill Blank, Subjective), batch import/export functionality, and content review workflows. Explain the question validation rules, knowledge point mapping, and difficulty scoring algorithms. Include the approval workflow for question administrators, status management, and quality assurance processes. Address the question task system for assignment tracking, content hashing for duplicate detection, and search functionality. Document the frontend interfaces for question editing, bulk operations, and administrative oversight. Provide examples of question creation, batch processing workflows, and integration with the exam paper system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Exam Administration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive exam administration documentation covering the complete exam lifecycle from creation to completion. Document exam paper creation, question assignment algorithms, and template management. Explain the exam publishing workflow, timing controls, and instruction management. Include the status management system (Draft, Published, Completed), question randomization, and exam scheduling. Address the integration with question management system, exam paper validation, and duplicate prevention measures. Document the frontend interfaces for exam creation, question assignment, and paper preview functionality. Provide examples of exam setup workflows, question assignment strategies, and exam administration best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Assessment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive student assessment documentation covering online answer submission, OCR processing, and automated grading systems. Document the answer submission workflow, file upload handling, and real-time validation. Explain the OCR integration with PaddleOCR for scanned answer processing, image preprocessing, and text extraction algorithms. Include the automated grading engine, manual review processes, and score calculation methodologies. Address the integration between online submission and photo scanning modes, quality assurance for OCR results, and fallback mechanisms. Document the frontend interfaces for answer taking, photo upload, and result viewing. Provide examples of assessment workflows, OCR processing pipelines, and grading system configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Book System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive error book system documentation covering automatic error detection, manual entry processes, and learning recommendation algorithms. Document the error classification system, mistake categorization criteria, and frequency analysis algorithms. Explain the integration with assessment results, question difficulty tracking, and student performance patterns. Include the study recommendation engine, personalized learning paths, and progress monitoring features. Address the export functionality, report generation, and integration with teacher analytics. Document the frontend interface for error book management, manual correction workflows, and student self-study features. Provide examples of error detection algorithms, recommendation strategies, and learning analytics dashboards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Teacher Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive teacher tools documentation covering class management, performance analytics, and student progress tracking systems. Document the class administration interface, student enrollment management, and course organization features. Explain the performance analytics dashboard, statistical reporting, and comparative analysis tools. Include the question statistics system, difficulty analysis, and topic mastery tracking. Address the student progress monitoring, grade distribution analysis, and individual learning path recommendations. Document the report generation capabilities, export functionality, and integration with administrative systems. Provide examples of class setup workflows, analytics interpretation, and instructional decision-making processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrator Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive administrator tools documentation covering system configuration, user management, and maintenance operations. Document the system administration interface, user role management, and permission configuration. Explain the LLM configuration system, model management, and AI service integration. Include the knowledge tree management, syllabus administration, and content organization tools. Address the system monitoring, performance metrics, and maintenance workflows. Document the backup and recovery procedures, data migration tools, and system health monitoring. Provide examples of system setup workflows, user administration processes, and configuration management procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Knowledge Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive knowledge management documentation covering curriculum mapping, knowledge node organization, and syllabus administration systems. Document the hierarchical knowledge tree structure, node relationships, and content organization principles. Explain the knowledge point system, competency mapping, and learning objective alignment. Include the syllabus management interface, curriculum version control, and content standardization processes. Address the integration with question creation, exam preparation, and learning path generation. Document the knowledge graph visualization, topic relationships, and prerequisite tracking. Provide examples of curriculum design workflows, knowledge mapping strategies, and content organization best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration & Extensions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive integration and extensions documentation covering third-party service integrations, plugin architectures, and extensibility mechanisms. Document the OCR engine integration with PaddleOCR, image processing pipelines, and text recognition workflows. Explain the LLM service integration for automated grading, content analysis, and intelligent tutoring systems. Include the file storage integration, cloud storage configuration, and media management systems. Address the captcha service integration, security mechanisms, and spam prevention. Document the plugin architecture, service registration patterns, and custom extension development. Provide examples of integration setup, service configuration, and custom service development guidelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation covering unit testing, integration testing, and end-to-end testing approaches. Document the backend testing framework using pytest, test organization patterns, and mock strategies. Explain the frontend testing with React Testing Library, component testing, and user interaction simulation. Include API testing strategies, database testing with test fixtures, and integration testing with external services. Address performance testing, load testing, and security testing procedures. Document the continuous integration pipeline, automated testing workflows, and quality assurance processes. Provide examples of test case development, mocking strategies, and testing best practices for both backend and frontend components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation covering Docker containerization, production deployment, and system maintenance procedures. Document the Docker Compose configuration, container orchestration, and environment management. Explain the production deployment pipeline, CI/CD integration, and automated deployment strategies. Include database migration procedures, backup and recovery processes, and disaster recovery planning. Address monitoring and logging configuration, performance optimization, and scaling considerations. Document the system maintenance procedures, update strategies, and troubleshooting workflows. Provide examples of deployment scripts, environment configuration, and operational runbooks for system administrators.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines documentation covering code standards, development workflows, and team collaboration practices. Document the coding conventions for Python (FastAPI, SQLAlchemy) and TypeScript (React) development. Explain the Git workflow, branching strategies, and pull request procedures. Include code review guidelines, testing requirements, and documentation standards. Address the development environment setup, IDE configuration, and debugging procedures.
